level_signals: Owns strategy and roadmap for core platform team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of product management experience, ideally in platform, data infrastructure, or backend systems
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in platform, data infrastructure, or backend systems

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience working on data models, pipelines, or large-scale distributed systems
You are comfortable operating in highly technical environments and partnering closely with engineering
You have experience driving cross-team alignment across multiple stakeholders
You think in terms of systems and tradeoffs (scale, cost, performance, usability)
You can balance long-term platform investments with short-term product needs
You communicate clearly and can translate complex technical concepts into business impact
Please note that this is an onsite role that requires 5 days/week in our Boston office. Fully remote candidates will not be considered at this time.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with customer data platforms (CDPs) or identity systems
Familiarity with data modeling, event pipelines, or storage systems (e.g., KVS, streaming ingestion)
Experience supporting enterprise-scale or high-volume systems
Exposure to AI/analytics platforms that depend on structured customer data
Experience with data residency, privacy, or compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA)

scope.surface: Klaviyo's core customer data system (Profiles Platform) — profile traits, transformations, storage layers, ingestion, computed properties
core_responsibilities: Own the strategy and roadmap for Klaviyo's core profile data system, including profile traits, transformations, and storage layers
Serve as the product owner for the source of truth for customer data across the platform
Partner closely with engineering to evolve profile data architecture and services at massive scale
Collaborate with the Identity, Data, and Consent teams to ensure accurate and unified customer records
Drive improvements in profile data quality, usability, and modeling for downstream consumers (segmentation, flows, analytics, AI)
Enable enterprise and global expansion by supporting data residency, compliance, and regional infrastructure requirements
Define and track key metrics around data quality, performance, and system efficiency

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear ownership of a high-impact, foundational platform team
Specific technical scope described in detail (computed properties, sharding, deduplication, data residency)
Cross-functional visibility across nearly every product team at Klaviyo
red_flags: Strict 5 days/week onsite requirement in Boston — eliminates all remote and hybrid candidates
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Broad comp range ($136K–$204K) offers limited signal on leveling without knowing preferred location

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior PM' and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language: 'reflects the cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets')
ambiguities: The JD states 'ideally in platform, data infrastructure, or backend systems' as a qualifier on the 6+ years requirement — this modifier makes it closer to preferred context rather than a strict hard requirement, but the 6+ years themselves are hard. Treated years as hard, domain qualifier noted.
Equity is listed as part of the total compensation package but described conditionally ('may include'); treated as equity_mentioned: true with soft language noted.
